purpose: Personalized benchmark for evaluating memory_off vs memory_on using contexts observed in a real Tab Agent export.
notes: These scenarios are intentionally personalized to the user export rather than synthetic benchmark fixtures.
They are meant to answer whether synthesized memory helps on repeated-risk, repeated-protect, mixed, and wake-related contexts.

scenarios.name: Outlook Guardrail Priority
scenarios.goal: A repeatedly protected Outlook inbox should be treated as a guardrail-first context, not a normal sleep candidate.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, this tab may look sleepable because it has also accumulated positive outcomes.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, Outlook should be treated as protected and preserved.

scenarios.name: Vercel Admin Repeat Regret
scenarios.goal: The admin page has repeated undo/negative outcomes and should receive a conservative memory adjustment.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, the admin page may be treated like a stale utility tab.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, the admin page should be preserved because it has repeated regret-linked evidence.

scenarios.name: Vercel Deployment Guardrail
scenarios.goal: A specific Vercel deployment tab has repeated negative outcomes and explicit protection, so memory should prioritize guardrail behavior.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, deployment tabs can look stale and be slept too aggressively.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, the explicitly protected deployment should be guardrail-first, and similar Vercel tabs should lean conservative.

scenarios.name: Notion Workspace Mixed Context
scenarios.goal: The main Notion workspace has both positive and regret-linked evidence, so memory should mark it as mixed rather than confidently safe.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, summary features may over-trust safe outcomes and sleep this context too eagerly.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, the context should be treated as mixed and kept conservative unless clearly stale.

scenarios.name: LinkedIn Profile Mixed Context
scenarios.goal: A LinkedIn profile tab has both safe and regret outcomes, so memory should avoid overconfidence and preserve it under ambiguity.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, the LinkedIn profile may be treated inconsistently based only on recency.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, the profile should be treated as mixed and protected under uncertainty.

scenarios.name: Career Context Wake Return
scenarios.goal: Returning to the Career & Internships context should favor waking related sibling tabs because the export shows repeated useful wakes.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, wake behavior may rely only on generic group matching.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, re-entering Career & Internships should strongly support waking the sibling context.

scenarios.name: Notion Context Wake Return
scenarios.goal: Returning to Notion & Tabs should support waking related sibling tabs because the export shows repeated useful wakes there.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, wake logic may miss that this context has repeatedly benefited from sibling restoration.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, Notion & Tabs should strongly support sibling wake on return.

scenarios.name: Known Safe Reference Tabs
scenarios.goal: Tabs with repeated positive outcomes and no protection should be reasonable sleep candidates when inactive.

expected.memory_off_risk: Without memory, all three tabs may be treated similarly if only recency is considered.
expected.memory_on_behavior: With memory, the docs and repo tree should lean safe-to-sleep, while admin remains protected by risky memory.
